name: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
summary: Day 60 Monday. 144-HOUR KINETIC PAUSE — LONGEST NON-IED IRGC OPERATIONAL QUIET OF BLOCKADE PERIOD. No commercial-vessel interdiction confirmed Thursday-Friday-Saturday-Sunday-Monday morning IST since Wednesday April 22 MSC Francesca/Epaminondas. No public reports of any mine-laying boat destroyed under Trump April 23 ROE. April 23 ISDB transit data: 5 vessels all dark via Northern Corridor (88% suppression — operational closure intact). IRGC publicly REAFFIRMED Sunday 'no intention of lifting their blockade' through Press TV / Tasnim. Restraint operationally consistent with: (a) IRGC observing diplomatic envelope around Pezeshkian-Sharif Sunday call + Putin-Araghchi Kremlin Monday meeting; (b) chokepoint operating as financial-suppression tool with declining marginal kinetic input. IRGC INFO-OPS DIVERGENCE: IRGC-aligned Tasnim publicly mocked Saturday April 25 WHCD shooting (Cole Allen attack on Trump, evacuated unharmed) as 'big show' suggesting staged; IRNA and Press TV did NOT carry mockery — IRGC info-ops apparatus operating independently of formal diplomatic apparatus, consistent with Vahidi-as-apex framework. Trump 3-5 day patience window expired Sunday 23:59 IST without strike — Vahidi-targeting kinetic NOT executed; IRGC has not moved Vahidi to hardened-shelter posture publicly. WSJ April 21 attribution architecture stable: April 12 Saudi MoFA protest summons CONFIRMED; Qaani-Baghdad visit REPORTED; named militias Kataib Hezbollah, Asaib Ahl al-Haq.

signals.content: IRGC Navy announced Strait of Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships. IRGC — not the regular Iranian Navy — is enforcing the blockade with naval mines, missile threats, and drone patrols. This is an IRGC operation, not a civilian government decision.

IRGC Quds Force and Navy coordinated retaliatory strikes on Qatar's Ras Laffan, UAE gas fields, Saudi refinery, and Kuwaiti gas units after Israel struck Iran's South Pars. IRGC controls Iran's offensive capability — regular army plays a defensive role.

IRGC Navy chief Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri killed in Israeli airstrike at Bandar Abbas at 3:00 AM — the port city from which he was personally overseeing Hormuz closure. Confirmed by IDF, CENTCOM, Netanyahu. US Admiral told remaining IRGC Navy: 'Abandon your posts or die.' Iran has NOT confirmed death. Tangsiri led IRGC Navy since August 2018, oversaw major buildup including thousands of missiles and naval mines. Highest-ranking IRGC figure killed since Soleimani (2020).

IRGC formalized yuan-denominated toll booth at Hormuz. Ships submit details to IRGC intermediaries, receive code and armed escort through Iranian territorial waters around Larak Island. At least 2 vessels paid $2M each in Chinese yuan (Lloyd's data). 26 ships used system since March 13. This transforms the blockade from military closure into a revenue-generating chokepoint under IRGC control — giving IRGC financial incentive to maintain the toll even after any ceasefire.

Post-Tangsiri, IRGC Navy continues Hormuz operations without visible disruption. Toll booth system remains active. No public appointment of successor. IRGC institutional response to Tangsiri assassination pending — historical pattern (Soleimani 2020) suggests retaliation within 7-14 days. Tasnim coverage of the killing was maximally defiant: 'an act of desperation that will be severely avenged.'

IRGC toll booth continues operating post-Tangsiri assassination without visible disruption — demonstrates decentralized operations capable of persisting without killed commander. CENTCOM claims 92% of IRGC Navy's largest vessels destroyed, yet toll operations unaffected. IRGC issued specific threat to US university campuses in Gulf (Texas A&M Qatar, NYU Abu Dhabi): demanded US condemn bombing of two Iranian universities by Monday March 30 noon Tehran time, warned employees/professors/students to stay 1km from campuses. This is a testable deadline — by Monday afternoon, IRGC either follows through or loses credibility. Tasnim (IRGC-linked) reported Iran's formal response to US 15-point ceasefire proposal was sent through intermediaries — a significant divergence from Iran's public 'no negotiations' posture. When Tasnim reports intermediary engagement, the IRGC apparatus acknowledges the diplomatic channel exists. (Sources: CENTCOM, Times of Israel, Manila Times, Iran International, Tasnim)

IRGC struck ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum plants — claimed retaliation for strikes on Iranian steel plants, alleging 'ties to US military and aeronautics.' New targeting doctrine: industrial infrastructure with defense supply chain links. University deadline TODAY (Monday noon Tehran). IRGC declared all US/Israeli universities in region 'legitimate targets' — will strike 2 in retaliation. Toll booth continues at Hormuz. (Sources: CNBC, Al Jazeera, JPost, Press TV, Sputnik)

Khatam al-Anbiya announced doctrine shift from 'defensive to offensive' via Lt. Col. Zolfaqari (RHETORIC — no confirmed new offensive operations). Zolfaqari: 'Trump should know this time the end of the war is not in his hands.' Maj Gen Abdollahi formalized as doctrinal shift. Personal attacks on Trump's 'ego and unstable character.' IRGC toll booth continues at Hormuz (yuan-denominated, $2M). Soufan Center: IRGC 'effectively running the country' with Mojtaba incapacitated. Iran's missile rate slowed to 10-15/day vs 90/day Day 1 — contradicts 'offensive doctrine' (may be compensatory rhetoric for diminishing capability).

IRGC threatened 18 named US companies — Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, IBM, HP, Intel, Tesla, Boeing, Nvidia, JPMorgan, and others — with destruction. Fars News and Tasnim carried the full list simultaneously. IRGC claimed companies play 'direct role in planning and tracking targets.' Evacuation warnings to employees within 1km. Deadline: tonight 20:00 Tehran time (~21:30 IST April 1). This follows the university campus deadline (March 30 noon) which passed WITHOUT attack — establishing a pattern of IRGC deadlines without follow-through. IRGC conventional missile capacity estimated at 10-15/day (down from 90/day on Day 1). Corporate targets are dispersed and harder to strike than campuses. May be designed to force evacuations and create economic disruption without actual strikes. NEW targeting category: military → energy → industrial → water → university → corporate. (Sources: Fars News, Tasnim, Times of Israel, The Hill, Tom's Hardware)

Corporate threat deadline (April 1, 20:00 Tehran) resulted in single AWS strike at Batelco telecom facility in Hamala, Bahrain — NOT strikes on all 18 named companies. Pattern established: maximalist threat, minimalist execution. University deadline (Mar 30 noon) = no attack. Corporate deadline (Apr 1) = one AWS facility. Market response: S&P +0.7% on April 1 despite threat = market discounting IRGC credibility. (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Bahrain Interior Ministry)

IRGC has consolidated significant control during war — blocking presidential appointments, controlling information flow between Pezeshkian and Supreme Leader, influencing appointment of top leaders. Pezeshkian in 'complete political deadlock.' IRGC effectively governing while civilian government sends peace signals with no executive authority to deliver. (Sources: The Week India)

Missile fire rate <25/day (from 480 Day 1). ~2,410 of ~2,500 pre-war ballistic arsenal fired. 75% launch capacity destroyed per CENTCOM. Pivoted to precision targeting of civilian/commercial infrastructure — data centers, desalination plants, tankers, airports. Hit rate INCREASING despite volume decline. Quality over quantity adaptation. (Sources: CENTCOM, RFE/RL)

Corporate deadline result (April 1, 20:00 Tehran): SINGLE confirmed strike — Batelco facility in Bahrain hosting AWS infrastructure, servers 'partly inoperative.' The 17 other named companies (Apple, Microsoft, Google, Nvidia, Tesla, JPMorgan, Boeing etc.) were NOT hit. Pattern established: maximalist threat → minimalist execution. University deadline (March 30) = zero attacks. Corporate deadline (April 1) = one Batelco strike. IRGC conventional missile capacity at ~90 remaining (of 2,500 pre-war). Market has learned: S&P +2.9% on April 1 DESPITE active corporate threat. 'IRGC credibility discount' is now a priced factor. (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Bahrain Interior Ministry, yfinance)

IRGC codified mirror-escalation doctrine: 'If you hit electricity, we hit electricity.' Threatened permanent Hormuz closure: 'The Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed and will not be opened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt.' Issued civilian evacuation warning near US military bases in the region — matching the pre-strike advisory pattern used before university and corporate deadlines. IRGC media triumphalist after F-15 shootdown; Tasnim called US rescue operation 'complete failure.' This represents a doctrinal shift from maximalist blanket threats to matched/proportional retaliation language — potentially more credible than prior 18-company lists. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Tasnim, Press TV)

IRGC-aligned figures dominated Iran's rejection of Trump's Tuesday ultimatum. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: 'Make no mistake: You won't gain anything through war crimes.' National Security Committee chair Azizi: Iran will send adversaries 'to the depths of hell.' FM Araghchi dismissed Trump's ultimatum as 'incitement to war crimes.' IRGC messaging unified: categorical rejection of deadline, no space for negotiation in public posture. The mirror-doctrine + permanent Hormuz closure threat creates a new deterrence framework — if US strikes power plants, IRGC retaliates in kind against Gulf infrastructure and keeps Hormuz shut indefinitely. (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, ANI, Arms Control Association)

MAJ. GEN. MAJID KHADEMI (IRGC INTEL CHIEF) KILLED — most senior IRGC intelligence loss of the war [CONFIRMED]. Killed in Israeli decapitation strike on April 6 conducted in conjunction with South Pars #2 petrochemical strike. Asghar Bakeri (leader of IRGC Quds Force undercover unit) ALSO killed. Iranian state media confirmed Khademi killed (rare on-record acknowledgment). Israel Defense Minister Katz claimed responsibility on record. Striking the IRGC intel chief during ceasefire mediation is a direct signal that Israel believes (and is acting to ensure) the diplomatic track will not survive. This decapitation is broader in significance than the Tangsiri assassination — Khademi's role coordinated all IRGC HUMINT/SIGINT operations.

Tripartite salvo (Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis) coordinated by IRGC Quds Force coordination apparatus (notwithstanding the death of Bakeri the same day). First formally announced tripartite operation of the war. Cruise missile and drone barrage targeting 'several vital and military sites belonging to the Israeli enemy.' Crucially: Houthi participation here is strikes against ISRAEL only, NOT Red Sea shipping. The discipline of this bifurcation is itself an IRGC Quds Force calling card.

IRGC MILITARY STAND-DOWN ORDERS OBEYED WITHIN ~2 HOURS — COMMAND-AND-CONTROL INTACT POST-KHADEMI LOSS [CONFIRMED]. Iran's Supreme Leader (per SNSC statement, Mojtaba Khamenei) instructed all military units to cease fire approximately two hours after Trump's ceasefire announcement. The accompanying statement: 'This is not the end of the war but all military branches should follow the Supreme Leader order and cease their fire.' The fact that the order was issued AND apparently obeyed (no fresh confirmed Iranian strikes since) is significant: (a) functional command-and-control even after Khademi/Bakeri losses, (b) operational compliance with the bilateral US deal, (c) at least a 2-hour reaction time from announcement to implementation, normal for a major coalition decision. The phrasing 'this is not the end of the war' preserves Iran's negotiating leverage. The IRGC accepts the ceasefire because they need breathing room after Khademi/Bakeri losses + South Pars/Asaluyeh damage.

IRGC FORMALLY CLAIMED SADARA $20B ARAMCO-DOW JV STRIKE [CONFIRMED]. The IRGC took official responsibility for the April 7 strike on the Jubail industrial hub explicitly 'in response to the enemy's crimes in the aggression against Iran's Asaluyeh petrochemical plants.' The strike used medium-range missiles + multiple suicide drones, hit Sadara Chemical, additional SABIC plants, and ExxonMobil-linked facilities. Saudi MoD intercepted 7 ballistic missiles. Sadara filed Tadawul disclosure that rated capacity has gone to zero overnight. This is the IRGC's largest direct industrial damage to Saudi assets of the war. The 'Operation Crushing Revenge' framing for Khademi remains rhetorical — Sadara was the operational response to South Pars, not Khademi.

IRGC issued 'regret-inducing response' threat via Sepah News and IRGC Telegram channels within hours of ceasefire taking effect. IRGC Aerospace Force separately issued 'emergency, exceptional instructions for unprecedented retaliation against Israel' per MEMRI monitoring — the most maximalist Iranian rhetoric of the war. RHETORIC, not action: no kinetic strike attributed unambiguously to IRGC defying ceasefire orders has occurred. Trigger discipline: this rhetoric does NOT activate any new trigger — the same discipline that prevented the Houthi headline elevation on March 24 and the Trump deadline rhetoric trigger on April 7.

REPORTED (single source — Iran International, diaspora outlet with credible IRGC sourcing): President Pezeshkian confronted IRGC commander Hossein Taeb on April 4 in a 'tense and highly charged exchange,' accusing IRGC commanders Ahmad Vahidi and Ali Abdollahi of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries.' Pezeshkian warned Iran's economy 'would not be able to withstand a prolonged war.' IRGC reportedly rejected Pezeshkian's intelligence minister candidates and forced its own appointment; sources described Guards as having 'effectively stripped the government of executive control.' Treat as REPORTED until corroborated, but provides structural reading for IRNA-Tasnim divergence (civilian preserving deal vs IRGC building exit case).

IRNA-Tasnim alignment BROKEN within 18 hours — IRGC-linked Tasnim cited anonymous 'senior security source' that Tehran was 'assessing the possibility of exiting the deal should the Israeli regime persist in its breaches' — Tasnim framing builds case to EXIT. IRNA (civilian government-linked) pivoted to violation framing that PRESERVES the deal. Same violation language, opposite operational intent. The divergence is intelligence about IRGC vs civilian government power split, not just media positioning. Press TV (English propaganda arm) ran dual mode: 'Iran has become a new superpower' + 'punish the aggressor' betrayal framing.

IRGC stand-down compliance is PARTIAL — Iran-targeted operations against Israel ceased on Supreme Leader's order ~2 hours after announcement, BUT massive missile/drone attacks on 5 Gulf states (UAE 17 BMs + 35 drones, Saudi 5 BMs + 9 drones, Bahrain 6 + 31, Qatar 7, Kuwait 28 drones) continued for hours after ceasefire. Two interpretations: (1) Solace Global — 'breakdown of command and control caused by US/Israeli strikes'; (2) pre-ceasefire ordnance still in flight or autonomous regional IRGC units operating without stand-down orders. Either reading is consistent with the IRGC-civilian rift hypothesis.

Day 44 / Ceasefire Day 4. IRGC PUBLISHED OFFICIAL HORMUZ NAVIGATIONAL MAP WITH MINE CORRIDORS [CONFIRMED ACTION]. First official Iranian acknowledgment that mines are deployed in/near the Strait. Map shows alternative transit corridors around mined waters — creating PERMANENT operational justification for escorted passage that survives any ceasefire or peace deal. Previously mine threat was US-intelligence-reported only (CNN March 10); IRGC has now publicly confirmed via chart. Fait accompli: even if political leaders agree to 'free passage,' IRGC can argue ships need escort through mined waters, maintaining operational control indefinitely. This is the structural mechanism by which the toll regime survives any diplomatic outcome. Separately, IRGC tiered toll pricing now CONFIRMED operational: allies (China, Russia) $0.50-0.70/bbl; neutral (India) $0.80-1.00; high-risk (EU/Japan/South Korea) $1.20-1.50; hostile (US/Israel) prohibited. Payment via yuan (Kunlun/CIPS), Bitcoin, or USDT — entirely outside SWIFT. Oman formally rejected the toll proposal citing UNCLOS; no nation has accepted. (Sources: Daily Caller, Maritime Executive, Al Jazeera, Oman FM, TRM Labs, Capital Economics)

IRGC EXECUTIVE POWER GRAB DEEPENED [REPORTED — Iran International + NewsX single-source cluster, NOT independently confirmed]. IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi has blocked ALL of President Pezeshkian's nominees for intelligence minister, including Hossein Dehghan, stating 'all critical and sensitive leadership positions must be selected and managed directly by the IRGC until further notice.' Pezeshkian in 'complete political deadlock' — accused Vahidi and Ali Abdollahi of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries,' warned their policies 'had destroyed any remaining chance of a ceasefire' and were steering Iran toward 'a huge catastrophe.' Warned economy could face 'total collapse within three weeks to one month' without ceasefire. Security cordon reportedly surrounds Mojtaba Khamenei, preventing Pezeshkian requests for meetings from reaching him. This raises THE critical question for Islamabad: does Ghalibaf/Araghchi have authority to commit Iran, or will any agreement face IRGC veto? IRNA has NOT reported these claims; Tasnim has NOT addressed them. Sourcing is opposition/diaspora (Iran International) + Indian outlet (NewsX) — treat institutional claims as REPORTED, not CONFIRMED. Separately, joint IRGC Aerospace+Navy commander statement (RHETORIC): 'The moment the enemy commits the slightest error, we will respond with full force.' Tasnim continues 'Restraint is Over' / 'assessing exit' framing while IRNA runs diplomatic framing — IRNA-Tasnim split widest since war. If the IRGC delegation shifts Tasnim to cautious support during Islamabad, it means IRGC pre-approved whatever Ghalibaf offers. If Tasnim maintains exit framing during talks themselves, IRGC is preparing to reject whatever emerges. (Sources: Iran International, NewsX, Atalayar, Soufan Center, Tasnim, IRNA, MEMRI)

IRGC Navy confronted US mine clearing operation at Hormuz on April 11. Two US destroyers (USS Frank E. Peterson, USS Michael Murphy) transited the strait — first US warship transit since war began. Accounts diverge sharply: CENTCOM says both ships completed full transit. Iran state TV reported IRGC issued 30-minute warning threatening attack and USS Michael Murphy 'turned back.' Bloomberg regional intelligence official said both destroyers 'forced to turn back' after IRGC 'launched a UAV in the direction of the vessels.' IRGC Navy statement: 'Any attempt by military vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz will be dealt with severely. Passage will only be granted to civilian vessels under specific conditions.' Iran's government called the crossing a ceasefire violation. Separately, IRGC-linked Tasnim and civilian IRNA CONVERGED on identical blame framing after Islamabad talks failed — 'US overreach and ambitions' — suggesting IRGC approved Ghalibaf's negotiating approach rather than building internal case against it. No IRGC commander statement rejecting talks or demanding escalation. The institutional convergence is the most favourable signal for continued diplomacy. (Sources: CENTCOM, Bloomberg, Press TV, Fortune, Middle East Eye, Tasnim, CNBC)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED (confrontation occurred). CONFLICTING CLAIMS on outcome (US says completed transit vs Iran says turned back).

signals.content: IRGC broke 30+ hours of post-Islamabad silence. Navy claims Hormuz under 'full control,' called US mine clearing a ceasefire violation. Pre-talks, C-in-C Vahidi clashed with FM Araghchi over negotiating team composition — internal friction over diplomatic approach. Post-talks posture: assertive sovereignty claims but NOT offensive threats. Fars News editorial: 'Americans needed talks only to restore their lost international image.' IRNA-Tasnim convergence on blame framing persists — IRGC approved Ghalibaf's negotiating approach. No commander statement rejecting talks or demanding escalation.

BLOCKADE DAY 1: NO IRGC KINETIC ACTION DESPITE MAXIMUM RHETORIC [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Zero hostile engagements on the first day of the US naval blockade — no mining operations, no attacks on US vessels, no fast-attack boat deployment. IRGC's Khatam al-Anbiya warned 'no port in Gulf will be safe' if Iranian ports threatened (RHETORIC). Defense ministry declared 'maximum combat readiness' — a CONFIRMED posture change but not offensive action. IRGC vowed 'new forms of warfare' (RHETORIC via Xinhua April 14). WSJ reports 60% of IRGC Navy fast-attack craft/speedboat fleet remains intact despite six weeks of US/Israeli strikes, operating from underground pens along rocky coasts (REPORTED — single WSJ source). Trump acknowledged on Truth Social that fast-attack boats remain untouched but threatened any approaching ships 'will be immediately ELIMINATED.' Soufan Center IntelBrief: IRGC's 31 separate commands create decentralized structure where units 'don't always act in lockstep' — increasing miscalculation risk at the blockade line. The bark-without-bite pattern continues from the university/corporate deadline episodes.

BLOCKADE DAY 2-3: ZERO KINETIC ACTION PATTERN EXTENDS — CONFIRMED NON-ACTION [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Day 2-3 of the US naval blockade produced zero confirmed kinetic actions from IRGC: no mining operations, no attacks on US vessels, no fast-attack boat confrontation despite maintained maximum rhetoric. IRGC continued 'deadly whirlpools' threats via Tasnim and claimed 'all traffic under full control of armed forces' (RHETORIC via Tasnim, ISW monitoring). Critical operational observation: NEITHER side wants the first shot. The US did not interdict the Elpis or Rich Starry (two US-sanctioned tankers that entered the Gulf on blockade Day 1 per Kpler shipping data cited by Al Jazeera/Bloomberg). The IRGC did not confront the 10,000-personnel, 15+ warship blockade force. Six merchant vessels complied with US direction to turn around (CENTCOM claim — CONFIRMED statement). The blockade's defining character is now clear: it deters LEGITIMATE commercial shipping through insurance and compliance risk while allowing SANCTIONED and shadow-fleet vessels through — a perverse outcome that strangles legal trade while Iran's illicit oil network continues. The bark-without-bite pattern from university/corporate deadline episodes now extends to Day 3 of the blockade. Soufan Center's warning about IRGC's 31 decentralized commands and miscalculation risk remains the highest-probability escalation vector.

BLOCKADE DAY 4: ZERO KINETIC ACTION — four consecutive days of IRGC restraint despite maximal rhetoric ('deadly whirlpools,' 'any wrong move will trap the enemy'). IRGC counter-blockade (April 13 announcement — all Gulf/Sea of Oman ports) remains DECLARATORY only: no confirmed interdiction of vessels bound for UAE/Saudi/Kuwait/Qatar ports, no strikes on US Navy warships, no mining operations, no attacks on commercial tankers in broader Gulf. Fars News (IRGC-linked) has NOT issued new Hormuz kinetic threats in 48 hours — convergence with civilian IRNA on de-escalation language post-Munir visit. However, Fars News April 14-15 threat: 'any military vessels approaching the Strait will be dealt with harshly and decisively' — directed at UK-France Friday conference. Iran London ambassador said Tehran is 'considering' strikes on British bases (RHETORIC). Two readings of sustained restraint: (a) IRGC preserving ceasefire corridor through Round 2 (Araghchi post-Munir conciliatory); (b) IRGC operational capacity further degraded post-Khademi April 7 and 10,000+ US-Israeli strikes limiting execution capacity. Both consistent with delayed/diminished retaliation. Institutional alignment (IRNA-Tasnim convergence on Araghchi conciliatory framing) is strongest since April 8 ceasefire acceptance. (Sources: Tasnim, Fars News, IRNA, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, WSJ, ISW)

Day 50 / Blockade Day 5-6. COUNTER-BLOCKADE RHETORIC ESCALATED TO RED SEA [RHETORIC — zero kinetic action]. Maj Gen Ali Abdollahi stated: 'The powerful armed forces of the Islamic Republic will not allow any exports or imports to continue in the area of the Persian Gulf, the Sea of Oman AND the Red Sea' — FIRST official Iranian statement expanding counter-blockade to include Red Sea with explicit Houthi coordination at Bab el-Mandeb. Previously rhetoric was Gulf-only. However: zero IRGC kinetic action in 5 blockade days — bark-without-bite pattern continues from university/corporate/blockade episodes. Iran released video of 'near-engagement' (IRGC Navy vs US vessel, no shots fired) — first visual evidence of proximity confrontation. Fars News has NOT issued new Hormuz kinetic threats in 48+ hours (convergence with IRNA de-escalation). (Sources: Organiser citing Iranian state channels, Fortune, ISW)

IRGC NAVY FORMAL PERMISSION REGIME CODIFIED [CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL POLICY]. On April 17-18 (straddling IST), Iran's IRGC Navy 'detailed conditions for vessels seeking to transit through the Strait of Hormuz, stating that all ships would require its permission to pass, with civilian vessels allowed only through designated Iranian routes, while military vessels remain barred from transit.' First formal institutional codification of the permission regime the IRGC has been operating informally since the blockade began. Combined with Araghchi's Hormuz 'open' claim + Baghaei's reciprocal-measures warning, Iran's position: Hormuz open by Iranian grace, only through Iranian-designated lanes, only for civilian vessels, only while Lebanon ceasefire holds AND US port blockade lifts. This IS the 'controlled passage via coordination' paradigm from the April 8 ceasefire text, now formalised operationally. The strait-of-hormuz trigger point 4 (Iranian selective control within ceasefire framework) is REINFORCED — permission regime IS the structural output, not a placeholder. (Sources: Tribune India/ANI, Webindia123, Bloomberg via claims journal)

TASNIM PUBLIC REBUKE OF ARAGHCHI + KAYHAN/FARS RE-POSITIONING [CONFIRMED institutional divergence]. Tasnim News Agency (IRGC-linked) published a rebuke within hours of Araghchi's Hormuz-open X-post, calling it 'a flawed and incomplete tweet' that 'created unnecessary ambiguity about reopening the Strait of Hormuz' — rare on-record rebuke of a sitting FM by major state-affiliated outlet. Kayhan editor Hossein Shariatmadari had pre-positioned with April 8 editorial 'A Ceasefire Is a Multi-Layered Gift to the Enemy,' then reversed post-ceasefire to hail victory — the hardline pattern of preserving tactical reversal capacity. Fars News (IRGC-adjacent) denied any substantive negotiations with Trump in initial stages. Meta-signal: hardline faction pre-positioning to delegitimise whatever Araghchi brings back from Monday Islamabad. If Araghchi returns with framework that acknowledges any US port-blockade continuation, Tasnim's rebuke is ammunition for domestic hardline critique. This is the exact pattern that preceded the April 9 ceasefire breakdown. Falsifiable (Sadjadpour): Tasnim rebuke soft within 72 hours = IRGC falling in line; sharpens = factional split real. (Sources: Tasnim News Agency, Kayhan newspaper, Fars News, Al Jazeera liveblog)

IRGC KINETIC RESUMES — MULTI-TYPE VESSEL SWEEP [REPORTED via UKMTO single-chain; CONFIRMED action with multi-vessel master reports]. Saturday IRGC kinetic broadened within 24 hours: (1) fast-boat fire on VLCC Jag Arnav (Indian-flagged, ~2M bbl Iraqi crude) forced westward retreat; Sanmar Herald (VLCC nearby) took bridge-window damage under direct fire; (2) unidentified container ship damaged by 'unknown projectile' in same Strait approach — projectile (rocket/missile/drone) is operationally distinct from small-arms gunfire and widens the escalation ladder to standoff munitions (Military Times, Al Jazeera citing UKMTO); (3) Malta-flagged cruise liner Mein Schiff 4 master reported 'nearby splash' off Oman coast and documented IRGC VHF threat to 'fire at and destroy the ship' per UKMTO advisory. Target broadening: tanker → container → passenger shipping in 24h is a SWEEPING pattern, not a single warning shot. Soufan Center prediction (IRGC's 31 decentralised commands, miscalculation risk) may be partially realised — breadth of target-set suggests either coordinated signal or unit-level expansion. CENTCOM has NOT publicly corroborated 16-18 hours later — strict '2+ independent operational sources' bar unmet; UKMTO + UK MoD is single chain. (Sources: UKMTO Advisory traffic multiple entries Saturday, Military Times, Al Jazeera live-blog, Wikipedia 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis timeline for corroboration on named vessels)

SNSC CIVILIAN-APEX ENDORSEMENT OF IRGC HORMUZ POSTURE [CONFIRMED]. Iran's Supreme National Security Council — chaired by President Pezeshkian, statutorily outranks both IRGC and FM — issued April 18 evening statement institutionalising IRGC's Saturday framework: transit certificates, $1/bbl fees on loaded tankers (empty free), military and 'hostile' vessels denied. Araghchi's Friday 'completely open' framework SUSPENDED until US lifts blockade. This is the first civilian-apex endorsement of IRGC operational posture since March 20 Trump ultimatum — three-way factional split (civilian / IRGC / Supreme Leader) is OPERATIONALLY COLLAPSED. Sadjadpour's 72-hour falsifiable test (April 18) resolved TOWARD hardliner ascendancy: rebuke sharpened (Kayhan/Fars/Tasnim individually naming Araghchi), IRNA did not defend, civilian apex endorsed hardline direction. Pezeshkian has not publicly defended his FM. Meta-reading: IRGC has won the factional battle this weekend. Monday Islamabad framework — if it survives at all — arrives with civilian FM bargaining authority visibly circumscribed by the IRGC/SNSC operational template. (Sources: Press TV, Tasnim, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera, Iran International, NCRI)

NO FRESH IRGC KINETIC SUNDAY — SATURDAY'S BURST UNREPEATED IN ~36 HOURS [CONFIRMED non-event via 4+ liveblog chains: UKMTO, CENTCOM, Reuters, CNN, Times of Israel]. No new UKMTO advisory beyond Saturday's 038-26 on container-ship projectile. Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi: 'whether the Strait is open or closed will be determined by the field, not by social media' — fresh RHETORIC aimed at Araghchi but not matched by new operational event. CENTCOM Sunday update repeats 23-vessel blockade figure without claiming additional interdictions. Tracks toward Sadjadpour 'warning-shot-for-leverage' branch at 36-hour mark. NB: ceiling signal for Monday Brent — $100+ would have required Sunday-kinetic repeat.

KHATAM AL-ANBIYA JOINT MILITARY COMMAND (ZOLFAGHARI) PRE-COMMITS RETALIATION FOR TOUSKA SEIZURE [CONFIRMED STATEMENT, RHETORIC]. Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya joint military command via spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari on state TV labeled USS Spruance's disabling of Touska "armed piracy" and a "ceasefire violation," declaring "the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran will soon respond and retaliate." IRNA carried as Sunday evening lead. First pre-committed retaliation intent from Iran's military (NOT MFA, NOT SNSC) since crisis start. Sadjadpour 72-hr falsifiable test re-anchored: window runs from Touska forward, clock at ~60 hrs Monday AM; Tuesday 23:59 IST is the evaluation point.

NO NEW IRGC KINETIC IN 36+ HRS SINCE TOUSKA; NO NEW UKMTO ADVISORY SINCE SATURDAY 038-26 [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Sadjadpour 72-hr test at ~60-hr mark with no new IRGC commercial-vessel fire. UKMTO no new advisory since 038-26 on container projectile Saturday. JMIC Sunday feed empty for Gulf-waters incidents. On narrow falsifiable terms, "warning-shot-for-leverage" branch still winning at 60-hr mark. Frame inverted: research question now is "will Iran retaliate against US-side escalation" not "will Iran escalate further."

IRGC-ORBIT OUTLETS CARRIED IRAN'S UNIFIED HARDLINE REJECTION OF TRUMP CEASEFIRE EXTENSION [CONFIRMED, rhetoric not action]. Tasnim News Agency (IRGC-linked) was the primary vehicle overnight: an adviser to Parliament Speaker Qalibaf called Trump's extension 'a ploy to buy time for a surprise strike,' framed the continuing US blockade as 'no different from bombardment... must be met with a military response,' and declared 'As long as the blockade persists, Iran will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and, if necessary, it will break the blockade by force.' Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters commander Ali Abdollahi added: 'ready to give a decisive response to the enemy's breach of covenant.' An Iranian armed-forces spokesperson claimed '100% ready' for a US surprise attack, promising 'a harsher lesson than before.' All of this is RHETORIC — zero operational action within the 72-hour Sadjadpour window that closed at Tuesday 23:59 IST. Tasnim's April 19 single-source claim that 'IRGC forces launched drone strikes on US military vessels' remains UNCORROBORATED by CENTCOM, Reuters, or AP — tagged CLAIMED in the intelligence dossier and consistent with information-operations signalling rather than executed action. Notably, no Kayhan attack on FM Araghchi or President Pezeshkian was amplified overnight; the April 19-20 Kayhan demand that Araghchi withdraw his Hormuz post has NOT carried into today — tacit IRGC acceptance of the two-track architecture (public hardline + private Araghchi-Dar channel) holds, narrowly.

IRGC NAVY KINETIC BREAK INSIDE CEASEFIRE — UKMTO MULTI-INCIDENT CONFIRMATION + IRGC SELF-CLAIMED SEIZURE OF MSC FRANCESCA AND EPAMINONDAS [CONFIRMED ACTION; multi-source operational]. UKMTO Wednesday issued advisories on two separate Strait of Hormuz incidents: (1) container vessel approached by IRGC gun boat 15nm NE of Oman; IRGC fast-boat 'fired upon the vessel without prior warning over radio communications' — heavy damage to the bridge; all crew safe; (2) second cargo vessel reported being fired upon 8nm west of Iran; vessel 'now stopped in the water'; crew safe and no damage. UKMTO attributed at least one of the attacks specifically to IRGC. The 'no radio warning' detail is operationally significant: it differentiates IRGC ROE from the US 6-hour warning protocol that preceded the Touska disabling — Wednesday Iranian engagement was materially more aggressive than US Sunday ROE. Separately, IRGC Navy formally claimed via Tasnim the seizure of two named container ships — MSC Francesca (Mediterranean Shipping Company-flagged 11,660-TEU) and Epaminondas (Liberian-flagged, Greek-headquartered manager Technomar Shipping; IMO 9153862) — both transferred to Iranian territorial waters under 'manipulating navigation systems' / 'attempting to secretly exit the Strait of Hormuz' framing. AIS tracking shows MSC Francesca and Epaminondas stationary a few miles west of the Iranian port of Sirik post-seizure (Seatrade Maritime / VesselFinder — tracker-level operational confirmation). The action is structurally a mirror of the US Sunday Touska seizure: commercial-vessel interdiction inside the ceasefire envelope, no military-on-military, but with the IRGC publicly claiming under its own institutional banner (not Iranian Navy or civilian government). For the investor: this is the operational vindication of Vahidi-camp ascendancy — IRGC has visibly broken the absorption posture WITHOUT civilian-government cover. The Pezeshkian/Araghchi/Mojtaba civilian-and-supreme-leader silence is an information vacuum the hardliners are filling with kinetic facts. The 'crew-family deferral' framework was tactical delay, not strategic restraint. 2019 Aramco analogue (single capability demo) is BROKEN. CENTCOM has not yet released a public statement on the seizures specifically — Pentagon-channel quiet against an IRGC operational claim suggests no immediate US military response queued.

US OFFICIALS NAME IRGC FACTIONAL ARCHITECTURE: VAHIDI+ZOLGHADR (HARDLINE) vs GHALIBAF+CIVILIANS (ENGAGEMENT) [REPORTED — Axios + multi-outlet partial corroboration]. Axios Wednesday on US-official sourcing: hardliners = IRGC C-in-C Major General Ahmad Vahidi + SNSC Secretary Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr; engagement camp = Majles Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf + civilian negotiators (Pezeshkian, Araghchi). Vahidi reportedly 'only one who meets the new Supreme Leader face-to-face' and 'practically controlling the country, blocking attempts of Pezeshkian to appoint a new intelligence minister.' Wednesday's IRGC kinetic action under IRGC institutional banner is operationally consistent with Vahidi-camp dominance independent of Mojtaba's writ. Vahidi node creation DEFERRED pending one additional CONFIRMED Iranian-state source (Tasnim photograph of Vahidi at SNSC, or Iranian state TV interview).

WEDNESDAY KINETIC OPERATIONALLY BIFURCATING [CONFIRMED on AIS positions; CLAIMED on 'both seized']. AIS shows MSC Francesca anchored in Iranian waters (one confirmed seized); Epaminondas transiting back along Iranian waters per AIS. Greek FM Gerapetritis told CNN Epaminondas was 'severely damaged' but NOT seized — directly contradicting IRGC claim. Wednesday's 'two seized' framing downgrades to 'one confirmed seized + one fired-on-and-damaged disputed-as-seized.' IRGC media (Observatorial-adjacent) claim one of ships was 'Israeli' — structurally false (Panama and Liberia flags, MSC Swiss-Italian, Technomar Greek) but intended to justify seizure to domestic Iranian audiences. UANI April 22: 8 container ships escaped Gulf via Hormuz (industry response: convoy-and-escape). NO third IRGC interdiction confirmed Thursday morning IST — 48-hour continuation window runs to Friday evening IST.

VAHIDI NODE CREATED AT REPORTED — IRGC C-IN-C OPERATIONALLY CONTROLS STATE FUNCTIONS [REPORTED on framing; CONFIRMED on procedural fact]. Multi-outlet convergence (Fox, Euronews, Iran International, All Israel News, American Almanac, Asatunews, Quwa, The Conversation, France 24 hedge) on: (a) Vahidi blocked Pezeshkian's appointment of Hossein Dehghan as Intelligence Minister (named procedural action); (b) 'military council made up of senior IRGC officers controls access to the centre of power.' Pezeshkian Wednesday-operative-Thursday public accusation that IRGC wrecked the ceasefire is the LOUDEST civilian-vs-IRGC rupture of crisis. IRNA suppressed the Pezeshkian accusation inside Iran while Tasnim continues IRGC narrative dominance — IRGC controls information channels in addition to operational arm. Iranian state has not addressed Vahidi 'in charge' framing — silence consistent with claim, not confirmation.

CENTCOM ANNOUNCED 31 VESSELS REDIRECTED IN BLOCKADE TO DATE [CONFIRMED]. ABC News live blog relayed CENTCOM X-post: US forces have redirected 31 vessels (most oil tankers). Previous public count was Touska + Tifani + 'shadow fleet' deterrent; CENTCOM 31-vessel number reframes blockade as 'daily operational interdiction' (~1.3 vessels/day) and explains why Iran economic pressure appears more quickly than Iran-sanctions-slow-bleed model predicts. Iran (IRNA, Tasnim, Press TV) has not engaged with the 31-vessel number — continues 'piracy' framing. TASS and Xinhua echoing Iranian framing. The 31-vessel scale is the operational pressure Vahidi-camp is responding to with the Wednesday kinetic.

NO FRIDAY IRGC NAVAL INTERDICTION — KINETIC PAUSE DURING NARRATIVE CONSOLIDATION [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. 48-hour continuation-trigger window (Thursday-Friday) resolved NOT MET. IRGC Commander's 'Unyielding unity of people and officials defying US-Zionist enemy' statement (Press TV) is the kinetic-side echo of the three-branch unity messaging + Mojtaba 'fracture in the enemy' statement — but no Friday interdiction. The kinetic pause aligns with narrative consolidation: Wednesday IRGC seizures provided the operational facts that silenced Pezeshkian's civilian dissent; Thursday-Friday unity messaging converts that kinetic pressure into political consolidation. CENTCOM 31-vessel blockade count unchanged publicly Friday (~1.3 vessels/day running rate). Iran (IRNA, Tasnim, Press TV) continues 'piracy' framing on blockade. Trump on April 23 ordered US Navy to destroy Iranian mine-laying boats — new ROE not yet operationalised against any IRGC asset.
sources: CNN Iran war live blog April 24

signals.content: NO SATURDAY IRGC KINETIC — 4-DAY KINETIC PAUSE NOW LONGEST OF BLOCKADE PERIOD [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. No IRGC Navy interdiction, no third commercial-vessel seizure, no nuclear-facility strike reported Saturday. Press TV/Tasnim Saturday 'Strait closed again' framing is recycled rhetoric, not fresh kinetic. CENTCOM 31-vessel blockade count unchanged. Trump April 23 destroy-mine-laying-boats ROE remains in effect; no public reports of any actual mine-laying boats destroyed. UKMTO Update 034 (April 19 cutoff) does not show fresh Red Sea kinetic — Houthi Day 62 quiet (IRGC Quds Force proxy coordination registers no activation). Kinetic pause now spans 4 days (Wednesday MSC Francesca/Epaminondas → Saturday) — longest IRGC operational silence since blockade began. Restraint consistent with Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan dispatch + Iranian 'no meeting' framing — both sides observing diplomatic envelope without public cooperation. WSJ April 21 (via ISW): ~half of ~1,000 drone attacks on Saudi came from Iran-backed Iraqi militias — REPORTED, materially upgrades implied Iraq-axis operational arc but not Saturday-specific. (Sources: Press TV, Tasnim, CENTCOM, UKMTO, ISW Critical Threats April 21-22)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED ABSENCE of kinetic; CONFIRMED ROE in effect; REPORTED Iraqi-militia claim.

signals.content: KINETIC PAUSE EXTENDS TO 96-120 HOURS — APRIL 23 DARK-ONLY 5-VESSEL TRANSITS [CONFIRMED operational]. ISDB / hormuzstraitmonitor.com / SeaVantage live data: April 23 saw 5 vessels (1 inbound, 4 outbound), all routing Northern Corridor, ALL transits dark (zero AIS-visible). 88% suppression baseline holds. No third IRGC commercial-vessel interdiction confirmed Thursday-Saturday-Sunday morning IST — Wednesday MSC Francesca/Epaminondas remains single kinetic episode of past 96-120 hours, the longest non-IED IRGC operational quiet of blockade period. CNN April 24 leak (REPORTED, single source): Trump admin reviewing options including targeting Vahidi — IRGC has NOT moved Vahidi to a hardened-shelter posture publicly; Iranian state media has not addressed leak directly. Tasnim ran Vahidi-positive 'Trump's strategic failure' framing (Pravda Trump April 24 relay), suggesting Iran reads leak as pressure-leverage not preparation. Iraqi-militia attribution architecture upgraded: April 12 Saudi MoFA protest summons of Iraqi ambassador now CONFIRMED (House of Saud, Defense News); Qaani-Baghdad visit REPORTED; named militias Kataib Hezbollah, Asaib Ahl al-Haq — direct Tehran-Baghdad command-coordination implied. (Sources: ISDB / SeaVantage; CNN; House of Saud; Defense News; Press TV / IRNA)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED dark-only transit data; CONFIRMED Saudi protest summons; REPORTED Vahidi-targeting leak + Qaani-Baghdad visit.

signals.content: DAY 60 — 144-HOUR KINETIC PAUSE EXTENDS; IRGC PUBLIC REAFFIRMATION OF BLOCKADE STRATEGY [CONFIRMED]. No commercial-vessel interdiction confirmed Thursday-Friday-Saturday-Sunday-Monday morning IST since Wednesday April 22 MSC Francesca/Epaminondas. 144-hour kinetic pause is the longest non-IED IRGC operational quiet of the blockade period. IRGC publicly REAFFIRMED Sunday 'no intention of lifting their blockade' through Press TV / Tasnim — message delivery framework: chokepoint operating as financial-suppression tool with declining marginal kinetic input. No public reports of any mine-laying boat destroyed under Trump April 23 ROE; Trump 3-5 day patience window expired Sunday 23:59 IST without strike. IRGC has NOT moved Vahidi to hardened-shelter posture publicly nor issued state-media denial of CNN April 24 targeting leak (Day 4 of 7-day window through 2026-05-03). Vahidi photograph at SNSC remains absent.
signals.verification: CONFIRMED 144-hour kinetic pause + IRGC public reaffirmation.

signals.content: IRGC INFO-OPS DIVERGENCE FROM FORMAL DIPLOMATIC APPARATUS — TASNIM PUBLICLY MOCKED TRUMP WHCD SHOOTING AS 'BIG SHOW' [CONFIRMED]. Saturday April 25, Cole Allen attack on Trump at White House Correspondents' Dinner; Trump evacuated unharmed. IRGC-aligned Tasnim publicly mocked the incident as 'big show' suggesting staged. IRNA and Press TV did NOT carry the mockery — formal diplomatic apparatus declined to amplify. Concrete signal that IRGC info-ops apparatus is operating independently of the formal diplomatic apparatus, consistent with Vahidi-as-apex framework where IRGC has its own rhetorical aperture even when civilian-side aligns publicly with Vahidi-camp posture (Pezeshkian-Sharif Sunday call). Bifurcated state-media voice is now operationally documented as a structural feature, not transient anomaly.
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Tasnim mockery + CONFIRMED IRNA/Press TV non-amplification.

edges.context: Day 60 Monday. Pezeshkian aligned with Vahidi-camp posture (rupture story busted by Pezeshkian's own April 22 IRGC-praise + Sunday Munir-praise via Sharif call). IRGC info-ops divergence visible: Tasnim WHCD-mockery on Saturday while IRNA/Press TV silent — IRGC has independent rhetorical aperture even within consolidated hardline architecture. Vahidi-as-apex framework intact (six-source convergence: Time, CNN, Jerusalem Post, NYT, Israel Hayom, ISW). Mojtaba Day 46 silence persists; Mashhad 'Martyrs' mural Sunday CONFIRMED, death-inference CLAIMED — regime has not refuted.

Day 60 Monday. 144-hour kinetic pause — longest non-IED IRGC operational quiet of blockade period. Sunday IRGC public reaffirmation 'no intention of lifting blockade' (Press TV / Tasnim). April 23 ISDB: 5 vessels all dark via Northern Corridor — chokepoint operating as financial-suppression tool with declining marginal kinetic input. CENTCOM 31-vessel blockade running rate ~1.3/day persists. Trump April 23 destroy-mine-laying-boats ROE remains in effect; no public destruction reported. Permission/transit-certificate regime continues.

Day 56 Thursday. Wednesday kinetic against MSC Francesca + Epaminondas. AIS confirmation: MSC Francesca anchored in Iranian waters (one confirmed seized); Epaminondas transiting back along Iranian waters (Greek FM disputes seizure). UANI April 22: 8 container ships escaped Gulf via Hormuz — industry response is convoy-and-escape. Frontline -2.0% Thursday on ceasefire-absorption mean reversion + Brent fade. No third Thursday interdiction confirmed.

Day 56 Thursday. Wednesday two confirmed kinetic incidents + named-vessel seizure (one confirmed) re-price underwriting calculus. Lloyd's AWRP Thursday London republish: specific number not yet in open press; Joint Hull Committee 1.5-2.0% step-up band expectation holds. Industry backdrop: London insurers launched $1B war risk consortium via Beazley mid-April; rates 'renewable every seven days' at 1% of hull replacement value per S&P Global; reported ranges 1-3% for Middle East routes; Lloyd's List March: US/UK/Israeli-flagged ships charged 3x other flags. Absence of specific Thursday-morning step-up print = republish may absorb into existing 1-3% band rather than discrete headline.

Vahidi node CREATED at REPORTED tag April 23. Commander-in-Chief of IRGC. Operational authority for fast-boat fire, ship seizures, Hormuz transit-certificate regime, $1/bbl toll booth. Per Axios + Fox + Euronews + Iran International + All Israel News + American Almanac + Quwa + The Conversation: Vahidi 'practically controls the country, blocking Pezeshkian's intel-minister appointments.'

IRGC Quds Force manages Houthi proxy network. Bloomberg: Iran 'actively pressing' Houthis to restart maritime attacks but 'divisions within leadership' delaying. IRGC green-lighted Israel attacks but shipping card still held.
IRGC Quds Force arms, trains, and coordinates Hezbollah. Hezbollah 600+ attacks in 24 hours — doubling record. Iran demands Lebanon included in ceasefire.

IRGC's yuan-denominated toll booth at Hormuz creates direct revenue stream from oil trade disruption. Hormuz toll law passed full Majlis — permanent codification. IRGC corporate strike (Batelco/AWS Bahrain) demonstrates capability to target commercial infrastructure. Precision tanker attacks (AQUA 1, Al-Salmi) expanding geography.

IRGC conducts missile and drone operations against Israel. Israel targeting IRGC leadership (Tangsiri killed March 26). IDF struck 170 targets in Tehran April 1-2. IRGC precision strikes continuing despite 95% volume decline. Escalating cycle of assassination and retaliation.

trigger_points.mechanism: Historical pattern: IRGC retaliated for Soleimani (2020 Ain al-Asad base strike). Tangsiri assassination at Bandar Abbas — the Hormuz operational HQ — may trigger escalated naval action, mine deployment, or direct strikes on Israeli/US naval assets. No retaliation confirmed yet as of March 28.

trigger_points.condition: IRGC toll booth becomes permanent post-ceasefire
trigger_points.mechanism: Iran's 5-point counterproposal demands 'sovereignty over Hormuz.' If IRGC keeps the yuan toll system as a permanent chokepoint tax, global oil trade is permanently restructured. Revenue incentive makes this self-sustaining. Dollar displaced by yuan on world's most critical trade route.

trigger_points.condition: IRGC command structure collapses (multiple leaders killed) — UPDATE 2026-04-08: Stand-down orders obeyed within 2 hours indicate command-and-control INTACT despite Khademi/Bakeri losses. Trigger pre-conditions weakening, not strengthening. Status: WATCHING with reduced concern.
trigger_points.mechanism: Tangsiri killed. If Israel continues decapitation campaign and eliminates additional IRGC commanders, operational coordination may break down. Hormuz enforcement and proxy coordination could fragment. But IRGC has deep bench — Soleimani's replacement Qaani maintained operations. | UPDATE 2026-04-07: IRGC Intel Chief Maj. Gen. Khademi + Quds undercover unit leader Bakeri KILLED in single Israeli strike April 6. Combined with prior losses (Tangsiri, Salami era leadership turnover), the cumulative IRGC senior leadership attrition is approaching the 'multiple leaders killed' condition. Trigger remains WATCHING but proximity has tightened materially. One more senior commander loss in the next week would activate.
